author: sproul
content: has anyone thought about unbundling attacks in ePBS yet? I think it is potentially relevant to the design of the postSignedEnvelope API: https://github.com/ethereum/beacon-APIs/pull/580#discussion_r2887819954

author: bharath.vedartham
content: Regarding the scenario which you have described in the comment, I think the builder would publish the block after the attestation deadline. So the builder could have a view on which block has gained enough weight and release the payload if the block that has commited to their bid has gained the most weight or is the most likely the become canonical?

author: bharath.vedartham
content: so I would release the payload only if Block A has gained enough attestations otherwise I can honestly withold my payload if block B becomes canonical

author: bharath.vedartham
content: I think the fact that the builder can now wait and see if the beacon block which includes their bids gains enough attestations before releasing their payload makes unbundling attacks difficult in ePBS?

author: sproul
content:
yeah that's a good point

do you think it's OK to externalise that check onto the builder, or should it be built-in to the BN's endpoint?

author: bharath.vedartham
content:
All the builders as far as i know rely on the beacon apis to broadcast their beacon block today, they don't directly gossip on the p2p network. 
A serious builder would also be super well connected in the p2p n/w and can detect equivocating blocks pretty fast. 
I think it makes sense to have a `broadcast_validation=equivocation` while broadcasting so that builders can be 100% safe from unbundling?

author: bharath.vedartham
content: A builder could also submit a slashing proof in this case I guess? but I don't think the builder would get the reward

author: sproul
content: yeah nah whistleblower rewards do not exist (it always goes to the proposer that includes the slashing)

author: potuz
content: We've been thinking quite a bit and the worst complications on forkchoice are due to it I believe. What would be your concern?

author: potuz
content: Ah need to read your comment

author: potuz
content: Yes the equivocation you describe in that comment <@602753420033785856> is specifically what led to `should_apply_proposer_boost` and similar changes where we now need to track equivocations live
